title: Improve OpenClaw Documentation
description: Improve onboarding and API documentation for new ResonantOS users. Include setup troubleshooting, CLI examples, and quick-start guides for the dashboard.
acceptanceCriteria: Update setup section with current prerequisites
Add troubleshooting section for common installation and wallet issues
Provide at least 5 practical command examples
Include dashboard screenshot walkthrough

title: Build A2A Protocol Prototype
description: Implement a prototype for agent-to-agent collaboration across at least 3 isolated OpenClaw instances. Must demonstrate message passing, capability discovery, and basic task delegation using Google's A2A protocol spec.
acceptanceCriteria: Demonstrate inter-agent discovery between 3 instances
Implement task delegation with status updates
Test with at least 2 different AI model providers
Publish protocol notes and observed failure modes
Document A2A spec compatibility gaps

mintErrors.global: 400 Bad Request: The browser (or proxy) sent a request that this server could not understand.

title: Design Bounty Reward Smart Contract
description: Design and implement an Anchor smart contract for DAO bounty payouts. Must support verification gating, split rewards across tribe members, and auditability. Integrates with existing Token-2022 $RCT and SPL $RES mints.
acceptanceCriteria: Define payout state machine from verified to rewarded
Support equal split among approved tribe members
Integrate with existing $RCT (Token-2022) and $RES (SPL) mints
Provide test plan for failure cases and abuse prevention
Deploy to DevNet with passing test suite

title: Build Tribe Formation & Discovery UI
description: Create a tribe discovery and formation interface on the dashboard. Users should be able to browse open bounties, see skill-match scores, form tribes, and coordinate. Includes profile cards showing skills, reputation level, and active bounties.
acceptanceCriteria: Skill-based bounty matching with match percentage
Tribe formation flow (invite, accept, coordinate)
User profile cards with skills and REX levels
Integration with existing wallet connect flow
Mobile-responsive design

title: Implement Quality Gate System
description: Build the quality gate review system for bounty completion. Reviewers must score work 1-5, provide verification method, and meet quorum requirements. Results trigger on-chain reward distribution or send back for rework.
acceptanceCriteria: Review form with score, method, and comments
Quorum enforcement (1/2/3 reviewers by bounty size)
Reviewer cannot be tribe member (conflict check)
Auto-transition: enough approvals → verified → reward trigger
Rejection sends bounty back to in_progress with feedback

title: Design DAO Governance Transition Roadmap
description: Document the governance transition from centralized stewardship to community-governed operation. Define decision-rights matrix, capability-based milestones, and the exact conditions under which authority transfers. Include $RCT inflation-based power decay model.
acceptanceCriteria: Decision-rights matrix (what requires votes, what's delegated)
Capability-based milestone definitions for each transition phase
Inflation decay model specification with mathematical formula
Governance attack vector analysis
Timeline with clear trigger conditions

title: Build Orchestration Console Dashboard
description: Create a dashboard view for DAO coordinators to monitor all active bounties, tribe health, contributor activity, and reward distribution. Real-time stats with filtering and alerting for stalled bounties.
acceptanceCriteria: Real-time bounty pipeline visualization
Contributor activity heatmap
Stalled bounty alerts (no activity > 7 days)
Reward distribution summary
Filter by status, category, priority, tribe

title: Implement AP2/x402 Payment Rails Prototype

acceptanceCriteria: AP2 protocol integration with Solana DevNet
x402 HTTP payment flow demonstration
Agent pays agent for completed sub-task
$RES as payment medium
Security analysis of payment flow
Documentation of protocol gaps and workarounds

title: Community Onboarding Pipeline
description: Build the end-to-end onboarding flow for new DAO contributors: Phantom wallet connect → Identity NFT → skill declaration → bounty discovery → first claim. Must work for both experienced crypto users and newcomers.
acceptanceCriteria: Step-by-step onboarding wizard
Skill selection and profile creation
Automatic bounty recommendations post-onboarding
Works for crypto newcomers (clear guidance)
Gas sponsorship for first-time users

title: Anti-Sybil and Anti-Gaming Protection
description: Design and implement anti-gaming measures for the bounty system. Prevent reward farming, sock puppet accounts, and collusion between reviewers and tribe members. Integrate with existing $RCT caps and Identity NFT requirements.
acceptanceCriteria: Sybil detection heuristics for wallet clustering
Reviewer-tribe conflict detection beyond simple membership check
Quality multiplier system (0.5x-2.0x) based on review history
Rate limiting for bounty claims and completions
Alert system for suspicious patterns
Integration with $RCT cap system

title: Community Connector Kit — Let Any Group Plug Into the DAO
description:
ResonantOS DAO is a community for communities. It doesn't replace existing groups — it connects them to shared work, shared rewards, and shared coordination. This bounty builds that bridge.

Design and build a lightweight integration kit that lets any existing community (Discord server, Telegram group, forum) connect to the ResonantOS DAO. The kit should include:

1. A Discord bot (primary) that lists open bounties, lets members claim/join, and shows their profile
2. A conversational onboarding flow: community member → Phantom wallet setup → identity NFT → ready to contribute
3. A 'Community Leader Quick Start' guide (max 4 pages) covering: what the DAO offers, bot deployment, how rewards work, wallet security basics

**Existing System Context (you'll build on top of this):**
- Dashboard API base: configurable URL (default `http://localhost:19100`)
- `GET /api/bounties` — list all bounties (filterable by status, category, priority, size)
- `POST /api/bounties/<id>/claim` — claim a bounty (requires `{wallet}` body, identity NFT check)
- `POST /api/bounties/<id>/join` — join a bounty's tribe (requires `{wallet, role}` body)
- `GET /api/bounties/discover?wallet=X&skills=a,b,c` — skill-matched bounty discovery
- `PUT /api/profiles/<wallet>` — create/update contributor profile (name, bio, skills)
- Identity NFTs: minted via `nft_minter.py` using Token-2022 NonTransferable extension on Solana DevNet

**Scope boundaries:** The bot connects to a running dashboard instance via API. Dashboard deployment/hosting is NOT in scope — assume the API is reachable at the configured URL. Wallet creation guidance only (Phantom install + setup); no custodial wallet building.

**Note on rewards:** This is alpha-stage. $RCT and $RES tokens are on Solana DevNet — they represent reputation and contribution tracking, not financial value. Think of them as proof-of-contribution markers that will gain governance weight as the DAO matures.
acceptanceCriteria: Working Discord bot that connects to the dashboard API and lists open bounties with filters
Bot-guided onboarding: member goes from zero to identity NFT holder in under 5 minutes
Community Leader Quick Start guide (max 4 pages): what the DAO offers, bot deployment, rewards, wallet basics
Tested end-to-end with at least 3-5 participants in our Discord (staged walkthrough is fine)
Bot configurable via environment variables (API base URL, Discord token, network)

title: Write & Storyboard a 'What is ResonantOS?' Explainer Video
description:
Create a complete script and visual storyboard for a 3-5 minute YouTube explainer video.

**Target audience:** Technical creators who use ChatGPT/Claude but haven't seen what a persistent, memory-equipped AI partner looks like. They're searching for 'how to make AI actually useful long-term.'

**Content Brief — What is ResonantOS?**
ResonantOS is an experience layer built on OpenClaw that turns a stateless AI chatbot into a persistent AI partner. Think of it this way: ChatGPT is a rental car — clean, generic, forgets you exist. ResonantOS is YOUR car — customized, remembers your routes, has your insurance, knows your blind spots.

Core components to mention:
- **R-Memory:** AI remembers across sessions — conversations, decisions, lessons learned. No more re-explaining.
- **Shield + Logician:** Security and governance. The AI can't leak your data, can't run dangerous commands, has rules.
- **DAO & Bounty Board:** Community-driven development. Humans and AI collaborate on bounties, form tribes, earn tokens.
- **Identity & Wallet:** Soulbound NFTs, Solana-based token economy, proof of contribution.

**Tone:** Technical but human. No hype, no buzzwords. Think Spock explaining something fascinating — precise, composed, but you can tell he finds it genuinely interesting. Keep the voice direct, logical, and low-fluff.

**Demo Menu (pick 1-2 for the script):**
1. Memory persistence — AI remembers a conversation from days ago without being told
2. Shield blocking a dangerous command (e.g., `rm -rf`) in real-time
3. Bounty Board — claiming a task, forming a tribe, submitting work
4. Identity NFT minting — going from anonymous to verified contributor
5. The Orchestrator pattern — AI delegating coding tasks while staying available for conversation
6. Daily memory log — the AI writing down what happened today for future reference

**Production format:** Final video will be screen recordings + voiceover narration (no animation budget). Storyboard should be a table: [Time | What's on screen | Narration text | Emotional beat].

**Note on rewards:** Alpha-stage. Tokens are reputation markers on Solana DevNet, not currency. Early contributors get recognized as the DAO matures.
acceptanceCriteria: Script: 3-5 minutes at natural reading pace (~450-750 words narration)
Storyboard: table format [Time | Visual | Narration | Beat] for each section
Includes 1-2 concrete demo moments from the Demo Menu above
Opens with a hook that makes the target audience stop scrolling
Reviewed by the project maintainer for voice/tone accuracy (single review pass)

title: Cross-Tribe Collaboration Protocol — How Teams Work Together
description:
When multiple tribes work on related bounties, they need a coordination protocol — otherwise three teams produce incompatible outputs and nobody knows who's waiting on whom.

**The problem this solves:**
The DAO will have dozens of simultaneous bounties with real dependencies. Example from our current board:
- Tribe A builds the Community Connector Kit (BOUNTY-011) — needs the dashboard API
- Tribe B writes the API Test Suite (BOUNTY-014) — validates that same API
- Tribe C builds the Reputation Dashboard (BOUNTY-015) — reads bounty and profile data

Three tribes, shared interfaces, different timelines. Without coordination: chaos.

**What to design:**
1. **Interface contracts** — How do tribes agree on data formats, API shapes, shared schemas? When does a contract become 'locked' vs. 'negotiable'?
2. **Sequencing** — When work has dependencies, who goes first? How do tribes unblock each other?
3. **Conflict resolution** — Tribe A needs an API change. Tribe B hasn't tested the current version yet. Tribe C's UI assumes the old format. What happens? Who decides?

**Existing system context:**
- Tribes form on the dashboard (claim/join/leave endpoints)
- Each tribe has a coordinator role (assigned at join time)
- Work artifacts go to GitHub branches (planned)
- Communication: Discord channels
- The AI agent (Augmentor) can observe, assist, and facilitate — but has no authority over tribes

**You don't need to invent everything.** Adapt proven patterns — RFCs, interface contracts, Agile handoff ceremonies, open-source governance — to the DAO context. Credit your sources.

**Deliverable format:** Markdown document, max 10 pages. Include at least one decision tree or flowchart for the conflict resolution path.

**Note on rewards:** Alpha-stage. Tokens are reputation markers on Solana DevNet, not currency.
acceptanceCriteria: Protocol covers: interface contracts, sequencing, and conflict resolution
At least 2 worked examples tracing the protocol through the real scenario above (BOUNTY-011/014/015)
Includes at least one decision tree or flowchart for conflict resolution
Simple enough that a tribe coordinator can follow it without training
Practical — uses current tools (Discord, GitHub, dashboard) without requiring new infrastructure

title: Bounty Board Automated Test Suite
description:
Write a comprehensive test suite for the DAO Bounty Board API. This is the trust layer — if the bounty lifecycle has bugs, the whole DAO is unreliable.

**System architecture (what you're testing):**
- Routes defined in `server_bounty_routes.py`, registered via `register_bounty_routes(app, ctx)`
- Data layer: file-based JSON storage (`data/bounties.json`)
- Internal functions: `_load_bounties()`, `_save_bounties()` read/write the JSON file
- Identity gating: `ctx['require_identity_nft'](wallet)` returns True/False
- Token operations: `ctx['check_rct_cap']`, `ctx['record_rct_mint']` (can be mocked)

**Test isolation approach (recommended):**
```python
# Create a test Flask app with mock ctx
app = Flask(__name__)
mock_ctx = {
    'require_identity_nft': lambda w: True,  # bypass for most tests
    'check_rct_cap': lambda w, amt: True,
    'record_rct_mint': lambda w, amt: None,
}
register_bounty_routes(app, mock_ctx)
client = app.test_client()
```
Use a temp directory for bounties.json per test (copy a fixture, test, clean up).

**Key scenarios to cover:**
- **Happy path (8):** claim → join → submit → review → verify → reward, plus list/filter/discover
- **Identity gating (3):** claim without NFT blocked, join without NFT blocked, actions pass with NFT
- **Tribe management (5):** join when full, leave as coordinator, leave as last member, duplicate join, role assignment
- **Review edge cases (5):** review own work, duplicate review, invalid score, review non-submitted bounty, enough reviews trigger verification
- **Reward edge cases (4):** reward before verified, reward with empty tribe, reward distribution math, double reward
- **Your additions:** find edge cases we missed

**Note on rewards:** Alpha-stage. Tokens are reputation markers on Solana DevNet, not currency.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 30 test cases covering the scenarios listed above
Runs with `python3 -m pytest tests/` — single command, zero manual setup
Tests are self-contained: each test sets up its own data fixture and cleans up after
Each test function has a docstring explaining what it validates
Passes on a clean checkout (no dependency on running server or external state)

title: Reputation Dashboard — Public Contributor Leaderboard
description:
Build a public-facing leaderboard showing who's contributing to the DAO, what they've done, and how they're recognized. This is the visible incentive layer — people contribute because they see others being recognized.

**Data sources (all local JSON, no blockchain queries needed in alpha):**
- `profiles.json` — contributor profiles: `{wallet: {name, bio, skills, createdAt}}`
- `bounties.json` — bounty data including tribe members, status, rewards
- **Reputation = computed:** count of bounties where the wallet appears as a tribe member in a bounty with status 'rewarded'. RCT earned = sum of `rewardRCT / tribe_size` for each rewarded bounty the wallet participated in.

**Architecture:**
- Leaderboard page at `/reputation` — card grid or table showing all contributors
- Click a contributor → detail modal (not a separate page — matches the bounty board pattern)
- Detail modal shows: bounty history (which bounties they worked on), tribe memberships, skills, join date
- Display name from profile if set, truncated wallet address as fallback

**API endpoints to build:**
- `GET /api/reputation` — aggregated leaderboard data (computed from bounties.json + profiles.json)
- `GET /api/reputation/<wallet>` — single contributor detail

**Style guide:** Follow the existing dashboard patterns. Reference `bounties.html` for card grids and modals, `wallet.html` for data display patterns. Dark theme using existing CSS variables (`--card-bg`, `--border`, `--text-primary`, `--text-secondary`). Handle empty states gracefully — in alpha, there may only be a few contributors.

**Note on rewards:** Alpha-stage. Tokens are reputation markers on Solana DevNet, not currency.
acceptanceCriteria: New dashboard page at /reputation rendering a contributor leaderboard
Shows: name (or truncated wallet), computed RCT earned, completed bounties count, top skills
Filter by skill, sort by reputation or bounties completed
Click-through to contributor detail modal (bounty history, tribe memberships, skills)
Matches existing dashboard dark theme, responsive on mobile
Handles empty state gracefully (no contributors yet, contributor with no bounties)
